hadiths.ar: 3964 - وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو إ ِسْحَّاق َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّارِب ِ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ن ُّز ِعَّث ْ مِن ْهُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّه
hadiths.en:
Al-Sakooni narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} from his 
father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, peace be upon him, who said that Imam 
Ali ibn Abi Talib, stated: 
"Hoarding during times of abundance is permitted for forty days, and during hardship 
and distress, for three days. Anything beyond forty days in times of abundance makes 
its owner accursed, and anything beyond three days in times of difficulty makes its 
owner accursed." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.165 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.267 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.159 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.3 p.114 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.392 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.423 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3963
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٦٣

hadiths.ar: 3965 - وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ إَّلط َّعَّامِ إَّلْمَّكِي لِ
hadiths.en:
Abu Ishaq narrated from Al-Harith, from Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib, who said: 
"Whoever hoards food, mercy is removed from him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.267 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.162 • Fiqh Al -Quran, 
Vol.2 p.54 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.187 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.138 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3964
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٦٤

hadiths.ar:
3966  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ إَّلي  ُّمَّالِى ِّ ق َّالَّ : ذ ُّكِز عِن ْذَّ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ع َّلاَّءُّ إَّلسِّعْزِ ق َّق َّال 
عَّلَّى  َّ مِن ْ ع َّلاَّن ِهِ إ ِن ْ ع َّلاَّ ق َّهُّوَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ رَّج ُّضَّ ق َّهُّوَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace be upon him and his family, said: 
"Measure your food, for indeed, the blessing is in the measured food."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.167 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.267 • Al-Ash’athiyat, Vol.1 p.160 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.95 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.440 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.13 p.280 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3965
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٦٥

hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abu Hamzah al-Thumali that he said: 
Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain {a.s} was informed about the rise in prices.  
Imam {a.s} said: “What concern is it to me if prices rise? It is upon Him {SWT}, and if they 
fall, it is upon Him {SWT}." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.81 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.267 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.6 p.321 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.397 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.18 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.57 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.17 p.431 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.46 p.55 • Awalim Al-Uloom, Vol.18 p.97 • Awalim Al-Uloom, Vol.18 p.142 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3966
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٦٦

hadiths.ar:
3968 - وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝ إ ِن ِّى  إ َّرٰإكُّمْ ن ِح َّي ْزٍ ۝   
ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Buy, even if it is expensive, for indeed sustenance descends with purchasing."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.150 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.268 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.4 
• Fiqh Al-Quran, Vol.2 p.57 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.125 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3967
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٦٧

hadiths.ar:
3969 - وَّ ق ِي ل لِلن  َّن ِى ِّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ لَّوْ سَّع َّزْب َّ لَّن َّا سِعْزإً ق َّا ِن  َّ إَّلْأ َّسْعَّارَّ ي َّز ِي ذُّ وَّ ي َّن ْق ُّضُّ ق َّق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم 
كُّن ْث ُّ لِأ َّلْق َّى إَّلل َّهَّ ي َّعَّالَّى ن ِن ِذْعَّه ٍ لَّمْ ي ُّحَّذِّب ْ إ ِلَّى  َّ ق ِي هَّا س َّي ْن اً ق َّذَّعُّوإ عِن َّاذَّ إَّلل َّ هِ ي َّا ْكُّلُّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ مِن ْ ن َّعْض ٍ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ إُّسْي ُّن ْصِحْي ُّمْ 
ق َّان ْصَّحُّوإ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated that Imam {a.s}, peace be upon him, said regarding the saying of 
Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic: "Indeed, I see you in prosperity" (Surah Hud 11:84) 
Imam {a.s} said: "Their prices were low." 
[REFERENCES] Tafsir Al-’Ayyashi, Vol.2 p.159 • Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.164 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.268 
• Al -Wafi, Vol. 17 p.397 • Tafsir Al -Burhan, Vol. 3 p.130 • Bihar Al -Anwar, Vol. 12 p.387 • Tafsir Nur Al -
Thaqalayn, Vol.2 p.390 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol.6 p.222 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3968
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٦٨

hadiths.ar:
3970  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى جَّمْز َّة َّ إَّلي  ُّمَّالِى ِّ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال وَّك َّلَّ 
ن ِالسِّعْزِ مَّلَّكاً ي ُّذَّن ِّزُّةُّ ن ِا َّمْزِةِ
hadiths.en:
It was said to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him and his family:  
"Would you set a fixed price for us, for prices rise and fall?"  
The Prophet {saws} replied:  
"I would not meet Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, with an innovation about which He {SWT} has 
revealed nothing to me. So leave the servants of Allah {SWT} to benefit from one another. 
And if you are consulted for advice, then give sincere advice."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.268 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.397 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.17 
p.431 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3969
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٦٩

hadiths.ar:
3971   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلص َّن  َّاحِ إَّلْكِن َّان ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم احِ س ِزَّإءُّ إَّلذ َّق ِي ق ِ ذ ُّل   وَّ 
س ِزَّإءُّ إَّلْحِن ْطَّه ِ عِز    وَّ س ِزَّإءُّ إَّلْح ُّن ْز ِ ق َّق ْز  ق َّي َّعَّو َّذ ُّوإ ن ِالل َّهِ مِن َّ إَّلْق َّق ْزِ  »
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by Abu Hamzah Al-Thumali, that Imam Al-Hussain {a.s}, who said: 
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Blessed and Exalted, has appointed an angel to regulate prices 
by His command." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.163 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.268 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol. 17 
p.431 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.432 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3970
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٧٠

hadiths.ar:
3972  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ذَّج َّلَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ عَّلَّى عَّان ِس َّه َّ وَّ هِى َّ ي ُّحْصِى  إَّلْ ح ُّن ْز َّ  ق َّق َّال 
جُّمَّي ْزَّإءُّ لأَّ ي ُّحْصِي ن َّ ق َّي ُّحْصَّى عَّلَّي ْك ِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Abu Al-Sabbah Al-Kinani that Abu Abdullah {a.s}, said: 
"O Abu Al-Sabbah! Buying flour is humiliation, buying wheat is honor, and buying bread 
is poverty. So seek refuge with Allah {SWT} from poverty." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.167 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.268 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 
p.163 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.92 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.438 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3971
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٧١

hadiths.ar:
3973  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلس َّكُّون ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
مَّن ْعَّهُّمَّا ي ُّورِب ُّ إَّلْق َّق ْز
hadiths.en:
It is reported that the Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon 
him and his family, entered upon Aisha while she was counting pieces of bread.  
Prophet {saws} said: "O Humaira, do not count, lest it be counted against you." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.269 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.163 • Al-Wafi, Vol.19 
p.272 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.446 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3972
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٧٢

hadiths.ar:
3974   -  وَّ ق َّالَّ رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ رُّج ْضُّ إ َّسْعَّارِهِمْ 
وَّ عَّلاَّمَّه ُّ ع َّص َّث ِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّى ج َّلْق ِهِ ج َّوْرُّ سُّلْطَّان ِهِمْ وَّ ع َّلاَّءُّ إ َّسْعَّارِهِمْ
hadiths.en:
Al-Sakooni reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} from his 
father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s}, peace be upon him, who said: 
"Do not withhold lending leavened dough and bread, for withholding them causes 
poverty." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.269 • Al-Ash’athiyat, Vol.1 p.160 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Vol.7 p.162 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.468 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.445 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.12 p.435 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.12 p.436 • Mustadrak Al-Wasa’il, Vol.13 p.282 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3973
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٧٣

hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace and blessings be upon him and his family, 
said: 
"The sign of Allah’s {SWT} pleasure with His {SWT} creation is the justice of their ruler and 
the affordability of their prices, and the sign of Allah’s {SWT} anger upon His {SWT} creation 
is the oppression of their ruler and the high cost of their prices."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Usul Al -Sittah Ashar (T. Dar Al -Shibistri), Vol.1 p.2 • Al -Usul Al -Sittah Ashar (T. Dar Al -
Shibistri), Vol.1 p.3 •Al-Usul Al-Sittah Ashar (T. Dar Al -Hadith), Vol.1 p.122 •Al-Usul Al-Sittah Ashar (T. Dar 
Al-Hadith), Vol.1 p.122 • Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.162 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.269 • Tuhaf Al -’Uqul, 
Vol.1 p.40 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.158 • Fiqh Al-Quran, Vol.2 p.52 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.395

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 75, H. 3974
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON HOARDING AND PRICING
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٥، ح ٣٩٧٤

hadiths.chapter: 76. CHAPTER ON JUDGMENT IN THE DISPUTE BETWEEN
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said regarding a man who sells 
something and the buyer claims that it was sold for a lower price than what the seller 
stated: 
"The statement of the seller is to be accepted if the item is still present in its original 
form, along with his oath." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.174 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.269 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.26 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.229 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.523 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.59

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 76, H. 3975
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON JUDGMENT IN THE DISPUTE BETWEEN
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON JUDGMENT IN THE DISPUTE BETWEEN
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٦، ح ٣٩٧٥

hadiths.chapter: 77. CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD
hadiths.ar:
3977  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ مُّي َّسِّزِ ن ْن ِ عَّن ْذِ إَّلْعَّز ِي ز ِ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ رَّج ُّل  إِس ْ ي َّزَّى  
ر ِق  َّ ر َّي ْث ٍ ق َّوَّج َّذَّ ق ِي هِ ذُّرْذِي  اً ق َّق َّال  عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ ي َّعْلَّمُّ إ َّن  َّ 
ذ َّلِك َّ ي َّكُّون ُّ ق ِى  إَّلز  َّي ْث ِ رَّذ َّةُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrates from Jamil ibn Darraj, who said:  
I asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about a man who purchased a 
property. He used to enter and leave it, but after paying the price and inspecting the 
property, he returned and requested the seller to annul the sale, but the seller refused. 
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, said: "Even if he had examined and inspected 
ninety-nine parts of it, and one part remained unseen, he would still have the right of 
option (Khiyar al-Ru’yah) based on inspection." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.270 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.7 p.26 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 
p.519 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.28 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 77, H. 3976
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٧، ح ٣٩٧٦

hadiths.chapter: 77. CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD
hadiths.ar:
3978 - وَّ ذَّج َّلَّ إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ سُّوق َّ إَّلي  َّم َّارِي ن َّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ إِمْزَّإ َّة ي َّن ْكِى  وَّ هِى َّ ي ُّح َّاصِمُّ رَّج ُّلاً ي َّم َّارإً ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهَّا 
«ي ْسَّ مِي ْلَّ هَّذ َّإ إَّل َّذ ِى  رَّإ َّي ْث 
ق َّق َّالَّ لَّه ى جَّي  َّى ق َّالَّ لَّهُّ ي َّلاَّب َّ مَّز َّإب ٍ ق َّا َّن َّى ق َّعَّلاَّةُّ ن ِالذِّر َّة ِ جَّي  َّى رَّذ َّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا وَّ كَّان َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّكْ زَّةُّ إ َّن ْ 
ي ُّح َّل َّلَّ إَّلي  َّمْزُّ.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrates from Muyassir ibn Abdul Aziz, who said:  
I asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, about a man who bought a container 
of oil and found sediment (dregs) in it. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If the buyer was aware that such sediment is commonly found in oil, 
he cannot return it. However, if he was unaware that such sediment is usually present, 
he has the right to return it." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.270 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 77, H. 3977
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٧٧، ح ٣٩٧٧

hadiths.chapter: 77. CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, entered the market of the 
date sellers. There, He {a.s} saw a woman crying while disputing with a date seller. 
Imam {a.s} said to her: "What is the matter?" 
She replied: "O Commander of the Faithful, I bought dates from this man for one 
dirham, but the bottom of the container was filled with poor-quality dates, unlike what 
I had seen at the top." 
Commander of the Faithful, peace be upon him, said to the seller: "Return her money." 
The seller refused, and Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} repeated the instruction three times, 
but the seller continued to refuse. 
So, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} struck him with his staff until he returned the money to 
the woman. 
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} used to dislike covering the dates to conceal their defects. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 5 p.230 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 3 p.270 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 18 p.737 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.18 p.110

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 77, H. 3978
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE OBLIGATION TO RETURN THE SOLD

hadiths.chapter: 78. CHAPTER ON ANNOUNCING THE SALE OF GOODS
hadiths.en:
Umayyah ibn Amr narrated from Al-Sha'eeri, who narrated from Abu Abdullah 
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, that Commander of the Faithful, peace be 
upon him, said: 
"When the caller (auctioneer) announces the price, it is not permissible for you to 
increase the bid. However, when the caller becomes silent, then you are allowed to 
increase the bid. Indeed, raising the bid is prohibited while the announcement is still 
being heard, but it becomes permissible when silence follows."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.271

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 78, H. 3979

reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON ANNOUNCING THE SALE OF GOODS

hadiths.chapter: 79. CHAPTER ON SELLING IN THE SHADE
hadiths.en:
Hisham ibn Al-Hakam narrated: 
"I used to sell Sabiri (a type of cloth) in the shade, and Abu Al -Hasan the First {a.s} 
passed by me while riding.  
Imam {a.s} said to me: 'O Hisham, selling in the shade is deceit, and deceit is not 
permissible.'” 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.271 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.466

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 79, H. 3980

hadiths.chapter: 80. CHAPTER ON SELLING MILK MIXED WITH WATER
hadiths.en:
Isma'il ibn Muslim narrated  from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s}: 
"The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) forbade 
mixing milk with water for sale." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.160 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.272 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.12 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.13 • Al-Wafi, Vol.17 p.467

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 80, H. 3981

hadiths.chapter: 81. CHAPTER ON DEFRAUDING THE UNAWARE BUYER
hadiths.ar: 3983 - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ عَّمْزِو ن ْن ِ ج ُّمَّي ْغٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} said: "Defrauding the one who 
entrusts you is unlawful gain, and defrauding a believer is forbidden."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.272 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.17 p.396 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 81, H. 3982
